title: An Autobiography
description: An Autobiography is a autobiography by Jawaharlal Nehru. Released in August 1941.
descriptionParagraphs: An Autobiography is a autobiography by Jawaharlal Nehru. Released in August 1941.

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a autobiography, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in New Zealand. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Released in August 1941. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

banningParagraphs: An Autobiography entered censorship debates as a autobiography associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
